title: Various handover processes in Wi-Max
author: Nandal,Deepak
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-zero
description:
WiMAX is a broadband wireless access (BWA) scheme 
based on IEEE 802.16. This is an emerging technology that 
aims at providing last mile access internet and data 
services. Since 2001, WiMAX has evolved from 802.16 to 
802.16d standard for fixed wireless access and to new IEEE 
802.16e (Mobile WiMAX) standard with mobility support. 
Mobile WiMAX introduces the most significant feature 
that is support for handovers which can be considered as a 
basic requirement for mobile communication system. Since 
this standard support all type of data and applications like 
mobile Voice over Internet Protocol (VoIP), multimedia 
video stream (IPTV etc.) or multimedia gaming and other 
real time, non real time services, so the handover schemes 
must be fast and efficient should avoid any packet loss. 
This paper gives an overview of handover procedure and 
types of handover supported by mobile WiMAX.
